citation.anchor: invalidate any ALAT entry at any

preconditions: Physical WBL memory holds one literal eight-byte GR20 payload and one literal single-precision FR21 payload
LD8.A r20 and LDFS.A f21 retire before the debugger checkpoint
No CHK.A executes before the source stops at bundle 0x40
inputs: One direct fresh-QEMU control execution
One source QEMU with a GDB software breakpoint at 0x40
One HMP savevm checkpoint and a distinct destination QEMU that loads it
Same-type GR20/FR21 and cross-type FR20/GR21 CHK.A probes
expected_results: Source and destination guest-visible checkpoints are exact apart from nonarchitectural current-slot diagnostics
Direct and migrated same-type checks each produce a valid hit-or-pessimistic-miss marker
Both direct and migrated cross-type checks take mandatory recovery
Both continuations reach terminal IP 0x100 without interruption
allowed_results: GR20 and FR21 may independently remain present or be pessimistically invalidated before either same-type check
The direct and migrated optional check outcomes need not equal each other
Internal replacement and transient lookup state is not a guest-visible oracle
cases.committed_prefix: The two advanced loads commit before the external debugger stop; the restore may retain or discard entries but cannot manufacture or retag one
cases.restart_action: A fresh destination resumes directly at bundle 0x40 and completes the same optional-hit and mandatory-cross-type safety graph

result_observations: source and destination direct typed traces

oracle.derivation: Addresses, payloads, GR/FR target identities, checkpoint IP, recovery graph, legal optional outcomes, mandatory cross-type misses, and checkpoint comparison are test-owned literals derived from Intel Volume 1 sections 4.4.5.3.1 and 4.4.5.3.2; no production ALAT or migration helper supplies an expected guest result

limitations: Reset and replay treatment remain separate ALT-007 atomic rows
The test exercises physical WBL entries; translated-address persistence remains covered at the serializer field level rather than this runtime path

citation.anchor: A new entry is allocated in the ALAT which contains the new ALAT register tag, the load access size, and a tag derived from the physical memory address

preconditions: The current ALAT wire version is one and contains thirty-two entry payloads plus one replacement cursor
The optional target-type subsection distinguishes GR and FR entries while an absent subsection defaults to legacy GR
Transient valid masks, GR masks, and reference counts are explicitly outside the wire image
inputs: Eight active legal entries crossing all GR and FR widths, low/high targets, physical/virtual address modes, distinct 64-bit addresses, and cursor 31
Invalid target types 2 and 255
Every lower/upper invalid GR and FR target partition
Every gap and outer invalid width partition for GR and FR
Invalid cursors 32 and 255 plus versions -1, 0, and 2
One inactive entry with arbitrary ignored target, width, physical, and address payload
expected_results: Every persistent field of all eight active entries and cursor 31 round-trips exactly
All transient masks and refcounts load clear
Every malformed active field, schema target type, cursor, and unsupported version is rejected
The same malformed field rows fail both incoming post-load and outgoing pre-save paths
The inactive arbitrary payload remains accepted because only its target-type schema metadata is live
allowed_results: Inactive non-type payload is preserved or ignored without becoming a live entry
The generic VMState layer may report additional transport framing errors before the ALAT callback
Error text is diagnostic and is not the result oracle
permitted_state_changes: The isolated destination ALAT persistent image on successful load
The isolated transient summaries, which must become zero
Host error state for a rejected save or load
required_unchanged_state: No CPU or guest memory exists in the isolated validator fixture
Rejected candidates cannot become an accepted destination image
expected_faults: EINVAL from the ALAT validator for each malformed semantic field or unsupported version
cases.committed_prefix: Successful v1 input publishes every persistent field atomically; any rejected semantic field prevents an accepted image and any rejected output prevents a valid checkpoint
cases.restart_action: No guest restart exists; the host save/load call returns success only for the complete valid image and EINVAL for each malformed candidate

result_observations: outgoing pre-save and VMState save return

oracle.derivation: The legal GR/FR target ranges, complete width sets, thirty-two-entry cursor boundary, field literals, invalid equivalence partitions, and version outcomes are declared in the test from the cited ALAT definition and frozen v1 wire contract; they are not enumerated from the production validator under test

limitations: Generic truncated-stream and I/O transport failures are owned by QEMU's common VMState tests
This atomic row validates the current v1 semantic field schema rather than compatibility with a future version

citation.anchor: invalidate any ALAT entry at any

preconditions: Every byte of an isolated CPUIA64State begins nonzero
The synthetic Itanium 2 reset helper is invoked exactly once
Every expected ALAT field is an independently declared zero literal
inputs: All thirty-two persistent entry slots
The replacement cursor
The transient valid mask
Both transient GR masks
All one-hundred-twenty-eight transient GR reference counts
expected_results: Every field of all thirty-two persistent ALAT entries is zero
The replacement cursor is zero
Every transient mask and reference count is zero
No compiler padding participates in the result oracle
allowed_results: Other CPU reset fields follow their separately asserted architectural or synthetic reset contracts
No guest-visible hit-or-miss result is inferred from this host state test
permitted_state_changes: The isolated CPU environment, including the complete ALAT structure
required_unchanged_state: No machine, guest RAM, or device exists in the isolated fixture
cases.committed_prefix: Reset publishes one complete empty ALAT image; no persistent or transient pre-reset field may survive
cases.restart_action: No guest continuation exists; the host unit returns only after exact reset-state comparison

oracle.derivation: Test-owned zero literals define every persistent and transient ALAT field in the frozen product reset contract; expectations are not obtained from the production reset helper or any ALAT invalidation routine

limitations: Real machine reset dispatch and RAM continuity are a separate E2 row
Intel permits pessimistic invalidation but does not define QEMU's internal transient summaries

citation.anchor: invalidate any ALAT entry at any

preconditions: A first boot advanced-loads physical GR20 and FR21 tags
A phase word outside all generic-loader data begins zero and is stored as one before reset
The guest waits at terminal bundle 0x90 before the host reset request
inputs: One live QEMU process
One HMP system_reset request
The exact stopped CPU image immediately after reset
Unseeded post-reset GR20 and FR21 CHK.A probes
Typed translation traces for both advanced loads and both checks
expected_results: The pre-reset checkpoint contains the integer payload, zero initial phase, and stored phase value one
Reset publishes entry IP, PSR zero, PR bit zero set, CFM zero, and all GRs zero
The RAM phase remains one and routes the restarted guest to the post-reset path
Both stale same-type checks take recovery and the guest reaches terminal IP 0x100
allowed_results: Either tag may be pessimistically evicted before the host requests reset
Nonarchitectural translation-cache contents may be rebuilt
Host diagnostic text and reset-event timing are not result oracles
required_unchanged_state: The HMP connection and QEMU process identity
cases.committed_prefix: Both advanced loads and the RAM phase store retire before system_reset; reset discards CPU-local ALAT state but does not roll back guest RAM
cases.restart_action: The generic loader restores only declared program/data words and the entry IP; the preserved phase word selects mandatory stale-tag checks

oracle.derivation: Program addresses, payloads, phase placement, reset fields, check graph, and terminal markers are test-owned constants; the expected reset image is not read from the reset implementation, and the stale-tag prohibition follows the declared empty product reset state

limitations: Reset of translated-address ALAT payload is covered structurally by the complete host reset image
Power-cycle and external platform-reset signal sources are outside this HMP system-reset row

citation.anchor: between different runs on the same implementation

preconditions: Record and replay start from identical loader-defined program and data images
QEMU icount record/replay is enabled with one test-owned event stream
The program establishes physical GR20 and FR21 tags before all checks
inputs: One record execution
Two fresh replay executions of the same event stream
Same-type GR20 and FR21 checks
Mandatory cross-type FR20 and GR21 checks
Complete harness architectural snapshots from all three executions
expected_results: Record mode emits a replay stream larger than its header
Every execution reaches terminal IP 0x100 without interruption
Both cross-type checks recover in all three executions
Both replays exactly equal the recording across every architectural field captured by the harness
The same-type hit-or-miss choices therefore repeat exactly
allowed_results: Each same-type check may either hit or pessimistically miss in the recording
The two same-type outcomes may differ from each other
Current-slot diagnostics are excluded because they are nonarchitectural success scratch state
required_unchanged_state: The recorded architectural result reproduced by each replay
cases.committed_prefix: The recording commits both advanced loads before the checks; each replay deterministically reconstructs the same instruction and event history from its initial state
cases.restart_action: Each fresh replay process runs from the initial entry to the same terminal checkpoint; no migration snapshot or debugger restore is used

oracle.derivation: The Intel-permitted hit/miss set and mandatory type separation are encoded as test-owned result classes; QEMU's public deterministic-replay promise independently requires both fresh replays to reproduce the recorded architectural class and exact harness snapshot

limitations: Replay from intermediate snapshots and reverse debugging are separate generic replay features
This row covers ALAT-sensitive deterministic execution without external device input
